steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content:
$$2x^2+13x-18x-24+27=2x^2-5x+3$$
The key move is distributing the subtraction across every term of $g(x)$, which changes $+18x-27$ into $-18x+27$. After that, the $x$-terms combine to $-5x$ and the constants combine to $3$. The simplified expression matches the correct answer.
